archetype: Archetype 2: The Performance Pro (NOC Terminal Variant)

layout_and_grids.spacing: Dense spacing (gap-4, p-4) to maximize data density on a 1080p TV screen.

page_structure.wrapper: h-screen w-screen overflow-hidden flex flex-col bg-[#0A0A0A] text-white font-mono
page_structure.header: h-20 flex-shrink-0 flex items-center justify-between px-6 border-b border-[#2A2A2A] bg-[#0A0A0A] z-10
page_structure.global_stats_bar: h-16 flex-shrink-0 grid grid-cols-5 divide-x divide-[#2A2A2A] border-b border-[#2A2A2A] bg-[#0F0F0F]
page_structure.main_workspace: flex-1 min-h-0 grid grid-cols-12 gap-6 p-6 overflow-hidden
page_structure.left_sidebar: col-span-3 flex flex-col gap-6 h-full overflow-hidden
page_structure.right_content: col-span-9 grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-3 xl:grid-cols-4 auto-rows-min gap-4 overflow-y-auto pr-2 custom-scrollbar
page_structure.footer: h-12 flex-shrink-0 border-t border-[#2A2A2A] flex items-center overflow-hidden bg-[#0A0A0A]

global_status.description: Massive indicator in the header showing overall network health.
global_status.classes: px-6 py-2 border font-bold uppercase tracking-widest text-lg flex items-center gap-3

client_health_card.wrapper: bg-[#121212] border border-[#2A2A2A] p-4 flex flex-col gap-4 relative overflow-hidden

elements.header: flex justify-between items-start border-b border-[#2A2A2A]/50 pb-2

offline_devices_panel.description: The most critical section on the left sidebar.
offline_devices_panel.wrapper: flex-1 flex flex-col border border-[#FF3B30]/40 bg-[#1A0A0A] overflow-hidden
offline_devices_panel.header: bg-[#FF3B30]/10 p-3 border-b border-[#FF3B30]/40 text-[#FF3B30] font-bold uppercase tracking-widest flex justify-between items-center
offline_devices_panel.list: flex-1 overflow-y-auto custom-scrollbar p-2 flex flex-col gap-2
offline_devices_panel.item: bg-[#121212] border border-[#FF3B30]/20 p-3 flex justify-between items-center

alerts_panel.description: List of active alerts below offline devices or sharing the space.
alerts_panel.wrapper: h-1/3 flex flex-col border border-[#2A2A2A] bg-[#121212] overflow-hidden mt-6
alerts_panel.header: bg-[#2A2A2A]/50 p-2 border-b border-[#2A2A2A] text-white text-xs uppercase tracking-widest
alerts_panel.list: flex-1 overflow-y-auto custom-scrollbar p-2 flex flex-col gap-2
alerts_panel.item_critical: border-l-4 border-l-[#FF3B30] bg-[#FF3B30]/5 p-2 text-sm text-white
alerts_panel.item_warning: border-l-4 border-l-[#FFCC00] bg-[#FFCC00]/5 p-2 text-sm text-white

stats_bar_item.description: Individual metric in the global stats bar.
stats_bar_item.label: text-xs text-neutral-400 uppercase tracking-widest mt-1

news_ticker.wrapper: w-full h-full flex items-center font-mono text-sm tracking-[0.2em] uppercase text-neutral-400

visual_enhancers.shadows: No ambient shadows. Use glowing colored drop-shadows only for critical elements.
visual_enhancers.scrollbars: MUST customize scrollbars to be thin, dark, with no gutter. Webkit-scrollbar styling required.

motion_and_interactions.micro_interactions: Since it's a TV dashboard, avoid hover states. Focus on state changes.
motion_and_interactions.state_changes: When a client goes offline, card border flashes red (animate-pulse).
motion_and_interactions.marquee: Use react-fast-marquee for the footer news ticker. Very slow, smooth drift.
motion_and_interactions.refresh_indicator: Include a visual 15s countdown timer or progress bar in the header to indicate the data refresh cycle.

accessibility_and_audio.contrast: Ensure pure white text on #121212 surface for maximum readability from afar.
accessibility_and_audio.audio: Implement a subtle but sharp audio beep (HTML5 Audio) when a new offline device appears or global status changes to Critical.

media.icons: Use lucide-react. Require icons for: Monitor/Server (device), AlertTriangle (alert), Plug (connector), Droplet (toner).

instructions_to_main_agent: Strictly follow the Dark Theme and Tactical Minimalism archetype. The UI must look like a high-end, professional NOC command center.
All fonts MUST be JetBrains Mono. Ensure the font is loaded globally.
Layout is designed for a 1920x1080 TV screen. Use 'h-screen w-screen overflow-hidden' on the root container so it perfectly fits the display without window scrolling.

The user mentioned 'custom CSS (no Tailwind on this page)'. If you are forbidden from introducing Tailwind classes in the actual React component files, you MUST write pure CSS in the associated stylesheet that strictly mirrors the Tailwind values provided here (e.g. padding, colors, borders). If you CAN use Tailwind, strongly prefer it.
The 'Offline Devices' section is explicitly stated as the 'most critical section'. Give it prominent real estate (left column, full height) and aggressive styling (red borders/glow) when active.
Ensure the 15s auto-refresh logic is implemented natively fetching from /api/tv/dashboard.
Add 'data-testid' to all major components (e.g., data-testid='global-status', data-testid='client-card-1').
Do not use Shadcn card components if they force rounded corners. Use raw divs with 'rounded-none' and 'border' to maintain the technical grid look.
